title: Department of Homeland Security Officer for Civil Rights and Civil Liberties Act
short_title: Department of Homeland Security Officer for Civil Rights and Civil Liberties Act
plain_summary: This bill makes changes to how the Department of Homeland Security protects people's civil rights and freedoms. It requires the department to hire a special officer whose job is to watch over civil rights issues and make sure people are treated fairly. The bill says this officer must be hired in a certain way and must have their own staff and money to do their work properly. These changes update an older law from 2002 to make sure civil rights are better protected.
last_action_text: Referred to the Subcommittee on Oversight, Investigations, and Accountability.
policy_area: Civil Rights and Liberties, Minority Issues

sections.title: Section 1. Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Department of Homeland Security Officer for Civil Rights and Civil Liberties Act.

sections.title: Section 2. Officer for Civil Rights and Civil Liberties
sections.text: This section changes an existing law called the Homeland Security Act of two thousand two. It makes three specific changes to Section seven hundred five of that law. First, it adds new language saying the Officer for Civil Rights and Civil Liberties must be appointed according to section one hundred three part d number three of the law. Second, it changes the letter marking of one part of the law from part b to part c. Third, it adds a new part b that requires the Secretary of Homeland Security to give the Officer permanent staff members and resources. These staff and resources will help the Officer do their job of protecting civil rights and civil liberties.
